differential_diagnoses: Enteropathy-associated T-cell lymphoma (EATL) or NK/T-cell lymphoma
Common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) with associated inflammatory bowel disease
Vasculitis (Polyarteritis nodosa or similar medium-vessel vasculitis)
Small bowel adenocarcinoma arising from chronic inflammatory or congenital pathology
next_steps: Surgical consultation for diagnostic laparoscopy and possible resection of aneurysmal jejunal segments
Full-thickness biopsy of the affected jejunal segments for histopathology and flow cytometry
T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies on peripheral blood and tissue
Mesenteric angiography or CT angiography to evaluate for vasculitis and vascular perfusion of the aneurysmal segments
Repeat bone marrow biopsy and aspirate to investigate the clonal NK-cell population and potential lymphoproliferative disorder
Comprehensive immunologic workup for CVID, including functional antibody responses to vaccines
Hold infliximab therapy pending definitive diagnosis to avoid worsening of a potential underlying occult malignancy
